failure_surface: Multiple execution attempts failed with SyntaxError due to malformed code wrapper format (using <tool_call>python instead of proper markdown code blocks), delaying successful task completion until the final attempt.
root_cause: The agent repeatedly used an incorrect code execution marker format that caused Python parser rejection, preventing immediate execution despite valid underlying logic.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: The agent should verify the correct code block syntax for the execution environment before submitting code, avoiding non-standard wrapper markers that cause parser-level failures.
evidence: conversation_tail shows 3 consecutive 'SyntaxError: invalid syntax' messages pointing at line 1 with <tool_call>python
The final successful attempt switched to standard markdown code blocks (```python...```) and returned 'Done'
The underlying Python logic (openpyxl operations, condition checks, formatting application) was syntactically correct once executed properly
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0], indicating no successful validation occurred until the final iteration

memory_items.title: Code Execution Wrapper Syntax
memory_items.lesson: Use only the execution environment's supported code block format; verify before sending code that the wrapper will not trigger syntax errors.
memory_items.generalization: When writing executable code, always confirm the target environment accepts the chosen code block delimiter format (e.g., markdown triple-backticks vs. custom XML-like tags).

memory_items.title: Early Syntax Error Detection
memory_items.lesson: Upon receiving any parser/syntax error during execution, immediately re-examine the code wrapper format before modifying the code logic itself.
memory_items.generalization: Syntax errors at line 1 often indicate submission format issues, not logical problems within the code body—prioritize checking execution markup.

memory_items.title: Execution Retry Strategy
memory_items.lesson: If code execution fails consistently across multiple attempts with the same error type, change the delivery mechanism before altering the implementation.
memory_items.generalization: Persistent execution failures of the same class suggest an environmental or formatting constraint rather than a code correctness issue.

edits.content:
## Code Execution Formatting
- Verify the execution environment's accepted code block format before submitting.
- Use standard markdown triple-backtick delimiters (\`\`\`python ... \`\`\`) unless specifically instructed otherwise.
- If line 1 returns a SyntaxError, check the code wrapper/marker format before debugging logic.
- Avoid experimental or non-standard code submission markers that may bypass the interpreter.
